introSection: Avalanche is a steel bobsled roller coaster located at Pleasure Beach Resort in Blackpool, Lancashire, England, UK. It was built by Mack Rides in 1988 and replaced the Cyclone.

sections.title: History
sections.content: Avalanche was opened on 22 June 1988 by Michael "Eddie the Eagle" Edwards, who represented the United Kingdom in the 1988 Winter Olympics ski jumping event. It was built on the former site of the Cyclone, which closed in 1987 and was relocated to sister park Frontierland Western Theme Park.

fullText:
Avalanche is a steel bobsled roller coaster located at Pleasure Beach Resort in Blackpool, Lancashire, England, UK. It was built by Mack Rides in 1988 and replaced the Cyclone.

Avalanche was opened on 22 June 1988 by Michael "Eddie the Eagle" Edwards, who represented the United Kingdom in the 1988 Winter Olympics ski jumping event. It was built on the former site of the Cyclone, which closed in 1987 and was relocated to sister park Frontierland Western Theme Park.



White track and green supports.
